## THE COMPLETE AI INFLUENCER SYSTEM GUIDE 2

*Source: THE COMPLETE AI INFLUENCER SYSTEM GUIDE 2.pdf*

THE COMPLETE AI INFLUENCER
SYSTEM GUIDE​
Join telegram for more sauce like this: t.me/mikoslab
INTRODUCTION
alright so you asked for the full system and here it is
this is literally everything i do to create ai influencers that look consistent across 100+ videos
and actually convert
most people fuck this up because they don't understand that CHARACTER CONSISTENCY
is 80% of the game
if your ai influencer looks different in every video, you're cooked
nobody will trust it, nobody will follow it, and nobody will buy from it
so pay attention because this is the exact workflow that's printing for me right now
PART 1: CHARACTER CREATION & FACIAL
ENGINEERING
THE BASE IMAGE GENERATION
first things first, you need to generate the base face of your ai influencer
and this is where most people already mess up
CRITICAL: DO NOT USE OPENAI'S IMAGE GENERATOR
it's absolute garbage for realistic people, the faces always look off and plastic
here are the tools i actually use and recommend:
●​ Flux - pretty good
●​ Imagen 4 Ultra - insanely good for realism
●​ Ideogram - solid alternative
●​ Midjourney is also great
●​ Nanobanana works well too just bad quality
when you're generating your base image, be SPECIFIC with your prompt
don't just say "attractive woman"
describe the vibe, the ethnicity, the age range, the style
think about who your target audience is and what would resonate with them
ENHANCING REALISM WITH ENHANCOR.AI
once you have your base image, run it through enhancor.ai
this tool takes your image from "good" to "holy shit that looks real"
it adds micro-details, skin texture, proper lighting falloff - all the subtle things that make a
face believable
this step is OPTIONAL but highly recommended, especially if you're doing image-to-video
workflows
PART 2: THE FACIAL ENGINEERING PROCESS (THIS IS
THE SECRET)
okay this is where the magic happens
most people skip this and wonder why their character looks different in every video
here's what you do:
1.​ Take your base image (after enhancor.ai if you used it)​
2.​ Upload it to ChatGPT- 5 (thinking mode) or Grok 4 or any smart vision model​
3.​ Give it this prompt (or something similar):​
"Analyze this face in extreme detail. I need a complete facial engineering breakdown that
describes every measurable feature. Include:
●​ Exact measurements between facial features (mm between eyes, eye width, nose
length, etc.)
●​ Eye shape, color, and characteristics
●​ Nose structure and proportions
●​ Lip shape and fullness
●​ Face shape and jawline structure
●​ Cheekbone placement and prominence
●​ Skin tone and texture details
●​ Hair color, texture, and style
●​ Any distinctive features or characteristics
Be extremely detailed and technical. I need to be able to recreate this face from your
description alone."
4.​ The AI will give you a MASSIVE breakdown - save this​
5.​ This becomes your CHARACTER CONTEXT PROFILE​
now you have a complete blueprint of your influencer's face
this is what allows you to maintain consistency across hundreds of videos when using
text-to-video generation
PART 3: TEXT-TO-VIDEO VS IMAGE-TO-VIDEO
here's something i've learned through hundreds of tests:
TEXT-TO-VIDEO with ultra-detailed prompts > IMAGE-TO-VIDEO for quality
yeah, tools like nanobanana and other image-to-video platforms have made it easier to get
consistent scenes
but the quality is heavily diminished compared to text-to-video
with my facial engineering process above, you can do text-to-video and maintain character
consistency while getting that premium quality
HOWEVER - image-to-video still has its place depending on your use case:
●​ if you need a VERY specific pose or expression
●​ if you're doing product shots where the character needs to hold something specific
●​ if you're doing transitions between scenes
both methods have pros and cons, and i use both depending on what i'm creating
but i love text-to-video done correctly
PART 4: THE VEO 3 WORKFLOW (SCENE
GENERATION)
alright now you're ready to actually generate scenes
i'm using VEO 3 as the example because it's the best tool for production work right now
but this workflow adapts to whatever tool you're using
STEP 1: GET THE VEO 3 META FRAMEWORK
go here: https://github.com/snubroot/Veo-3-Meta-Framework
this framework is INSANELY good at structuring prompts for veo 3
STEP 2: SET UP YOUR CLAUDE PROJECT
●​ create a new claude project
●​ copy the ENTIRE veo 3 meta framework
●​ paste it into the project knowledge base (NOT just the chat)
●​ this allows claude to actually understand and use the framework properly
DO NOT USE CHATGPT FOR THIS
chatgpt is terrible for scriptwriting and it's a lazy LLM for creative work
claude is way better at following complex frameworks and giving you detailed outputs
STEP 3: UPLOAD YOUR CHARACTER CONTEXT PROFILE
remember that facial engineering breakdown you got earlier?
upload that to your claude project as well
now claude knows EXACTLY what your character looks like
STEP 4: GENERATE YOUR PROMPTS
in the chat, tell claude what scene you want to create
"i need a scene where [character name] is sitting in a coffee shop, looking at her phone and
smiling, then looking up at the camera with excitement"
claude will use the framework + your character profile to generate a FULLY DETAILED
prompt for veo 3
the prompt will include:
●​ camera angles and movements
●​ lighting setup
●​ your character's facial features (pulled from your context profile)
●​ environment details
●​ mood and atmosphere
●​ everything veo 3 needs to generate quality output
PART 5: THE QUALITY SECRET (8K SHOT
PROMPTING)
here's something most people don't know:
VEO 3 FOLLOWS CAMERA PROMPTING STRUCTURES EXTREMELY WELL
the github framework i mentioned doesn't include 4k or 8k shot prompting by default
so before you send your final prompt to veo 3, you need to add this:
in your claude chat, tell it:
"make sure this scene is shot on a [SPECIFIC 8K CAMERA MODEL] with ultra-detailed 8K
resolution"
have it specify the actual camera - like "RED Komodo 6K" or "ARRI Alexa LF" or "Sony
Venice 8K"
this COMPLETELY changes the output quality
veo 3 understands cinematography language and will adjust the rendering to match that
camera's characteristics
8K SHOT PROMPTING CHANGED EVERYTHING FOR ME
suddenly my videos went from "pretty good" to "wait this looks like a real commercial"
PART 6: POST-PRODUCTION (HIDING AI TELLS)
okay your video is generated, now you need to make it look even more real
GRAINS & FILTERS
i use CapCut for all my post-production work
it's free, it's fast, and it has everything you need
add a subtle film grain overlay - this is KEY
ai-generated videos are often TOO clean and perfect
real footage has grain, imperfections, slight color shifts
play around with different grain intensities and filter combinations until it feels more organic
TOPAZ UPSCALER (USE CAREFULLY)
Topaz Video AI can upscale your footage but you need to be careful
if you overdo it, you'll get that weird cartoon/over-processed look
here's my approach:
●​ upscale factor: 1.25x to 1.75x (NO HIGHER)
●​ this gives you a slight quality boost without destroying the natural look
●​ combined with 8k shot prompting in your original generation, this is usually enough
test different factors on a short clip before processing your entire video
PART 7: NATURAL DIALOGUE & SPEECH (EMOTIONAL
BLOCKS)
if your ai influencer is speaking, this is CRITICAL
most ai voices sound robotic because they deliver every word with the same energy
here's the fix: EMOTIONAL BLOCK CUES
in your veo 3 prompt, structure your dialogue like this:
[Happy] Hello, [surprised] my [excited] name [joyful] is [proud] Sarah [confident] and [eager]
this [fascinated] is [focused] the [curious] secret [inspired] to [determined] viral content!
you're literally telling the AI what emotion to convey on EVERY SINGLE WORD
veo 3 follows this extremely well
the result? dialogue that sounds natural, energetic, and human
people won't be able to tell it's ai-generated
PART 8: MAINTAINING CONSISTENCY ACROSS 100+
VIDEOS
now that you have the workflow, here's how to scale it:
YOUR CHARACTER BIBLE
create a document with:
●​ your character context profile (the facial engineering breakdown)
●​ their personality traits
●​ their speaking style
●​ their typical outfits/style
●​ their backstory (if relevant)
●​ common scenarios they appear in
every time you generate a new video, reference this document
BATCH GENERATION
with this system, you can generate multiple scenes in parallel:
1.​ plan out 10-20 different scenes
2.​ have claude generate prompts for all of them (using your character profile)
3.​ queue them up in veo 3
4.​ process in post-production in batches
i can pump out 20-30 videos a week with this workflow
TESTING & ITERATION
not every video will be perfect on the first try
the key is to test, save what works, and iterate on what doesn't
keep a swipe file of your best prompts and best outputs
over time you'll develop an intuition for what works for YOUR specific character
PART 9: THE TOOLS BREAKDOWN
here's everything i actually use:
FREE TOOLS:
●​ Claude (for prompt engineering with the veo framework)
●​ CapCut (for post-production editing)
●​ Flux has a free tier ​
Imagen 4 Ultra (alternative image generator)​
Nanobanana (for image-to-video when needed)​
PAID TOOLS:
●​ VEO 3 (best text-to-video, worth every penny)
●​ Enhancor.ai (for image enhancement)
●​ Topaz Video AI (for upscaling - optional but nice to have)
●​ ChatGPT Pro (for the thinking mode, helpful for facial analysis)
you don't need ALL of these to start
the core workflow is: image generator → facial analysis → claude + veo 3 framework → veo
3 → capcut
FINAL THOUGHTS
this is literally the exact system i use every single day
it's not some theoretical bullshit, this is PROVEN and PRINTING
the biggest mistake people make is skipping the facial engineering step
they generate a pretty face, throw it into veo, and wonder why it looks different every time
you need that CHARACTER CONTEXT PROFILE
once you have that, everything else falls into place
and remember: this is a skill that gets better with reps
your first 10 videos might not be perfect
but by video 50? you'll be pumping out content that looks indistinguishable from real footage
WHAT'S NEXT
i'll be dropping more advanced scaling strategies in the telegram
things like:
●​ running multiple ai influencers simultaneously
●​ monetization strategies that are working right now
●​ how to build actual communities around ai characters
●​ advanced editing techniques for different niches
join here: t.me/mikoslab
don't sleep on this
ai influencers are just getting started and most people still don't know how to do this properly
now you do. go execute

## THE COMPLETE AI INFLUENCER SYSTEM GUIDE

*Source: THE COMPLETE AI INFLUENCER SYSTEM GUIDE.pdf*

THE COMPLETE AI INFLUENCER
SYSTEM GUIDE​
Join telegram for more sauce like this: t.me/mikoslab
INTRODUCTION
alright so you asked for the full system and here it is
this is literally everything i do to create ai influencers that look consistent across 100+ videos
and actually convert
most people fuck this up because they don't understand that CHARACTER CONSISTENCY
is 80% of the game
if your ai influencer looks different in every video, you're cooked
nobody will trust it, nobody will follow it, and nobody will buy from it
so pay attention because this is the exact workflow that's printing for me right now
PART 1: CHARACTER CREATION & FACIAL
ENGINEERING
THE BASE IMAGE GENERATION
first things first, you need to generate the base face of your ai influencer
and this is where most people already mess up
CRITICAL: DO NOT USE OPENAI'S IMAGE GENERATOR
it's absolute garbage for realistic people, the faces always look off and plastic
here are the tools i actually use and recommend:
●​ Flux - pretty good
●​ Imagen 4 Ultra - insanely good for realism
●​ Ideogram - solid alternative
●​ Midjourney is also great
●​ Nanobanana works well too just bad quality
when you're generating your base image, be SPECIFIC with your prompt
don't just say "attractive woman"
describe the vibe, the ethnicity, the age range, the style
think about who your target audience is and what would resonate with them
ENHANCING REALISM WITH ENHANCOR.AI
once you have your base image, run it through enhancor.ai
this tool takes your image from "good" to "holy shit that looks real"
it adds micro-details, skin texture, proper lighting falloff - all the subtle things that make a
face believable
this step is OPTIONAL but highly recommended, especially if you're doing image-to-video
workflows
PART 2: THE FACIAL ENGINEERING PROCESS (THIS IS
THE SECRET)
okay this is where the magic happens
most people skip this and wonder why their character looks different in every video
here's what you do:
1.​ Take your base image (after enhancor.ai if you used it)​
2.​ Upload it to ChatGPT- 5 (thinking mode) or Grok 4 or any smart vision model​
3.​ Give it this prompt (or something similar):​
"Analyze this face in extreme detail. I need a complete facial engineering breakdown that
describes every measurable feature. Include:
●​ Exact measurements between facial features (mm between eyes, eye width, nose
length, etc.)
●​ Eye shape, color, and characteristics
●​ Nose structure and proportions
●​ Lip shape and fullness
●​ Face shape and jawline structure
●​ Cheekbone placement and prominence
●​ Skin tone and texture details
●​ Hair color, texture, and style
●​ Any distinctive features or characteristics
Be extremely detailed and technical. I need to be able to recreate this face from your
description alone."
4.​ The AI will give you a MASSIVE breakdown - save this​
5.​ This becomes your CHARACTER CONTEXT PROFILE​
now you have a complete blueprint of your influencer's face
this is what allows you to maintain consistency across hundreds of videos when using
text-to-video generation
PART 3: TEXT-TO-VIDEO VS IMAGE-TO-VIDEO
here's something i've learned through hundreds of tests:
TEXT-TO-VIDEO with ultra-detailed prompts > IMAGE-TO-VIDEO for quality
yeah, tools like nanobanana and other image-to-video platforms have made it easier to get
consistent scenes
but the quality is heavily diminished compared to text-to-video
with my facial engineering process above, you can do text-to-video and maintain character
consistency while getting that premium quality
HOWEVER - image-to-video still has its place depending on your use case:
●​ if you need a VERY specific pose or expression
●​ if you're doing product shots where the character needs to hold something specific
●​ if you're doing transitions between scenes
both methods have pros and cons, and i use both depending on what i'm creating
but i love text-to-video done correctly
PART 4: THE VEO 3 WORKFLOW (SCENE
GENERATION)
alright now you're ready to actually generate scenes
i'm using VEO 3 as the example because it's the best tool for production work right now
but this workflow adapts to whatever tool you're using
STEP 1: GET THE VEO 3 META FRAMEWORK
go here: https://github.com/snubroot/Veo-3-Meta-Framework
this framework is INSANELY good at structuring prompts for veo 3
STEP 2: SET UP YOUR CLAUDE PROJECT
●​ create a new claude project
●​ copy the ENTIRE veo 3 meta framework
●​ paste it into the project knowledge base (NOT just the chat)
●​ this allows claude to actually understand and use the framework properly
DO NOT USE CHATGPT FOR THIS
chatgpt is terrible for scriptwriting and it's a lazy LLM for creative work
claude is way better at following complex frameworks and giving you detailed outputs
STEP 3: UPLOAD YOUR CHARACTER CONTEXT PROFILE
remember that facial engineering breakdown you got earlier?
upload that to your claude project as well
now claude knows EXACTLY what your character looks like
STEP 4: GENERATE YOUR PROMPTS
in the chat, tell claude what scene you want to create
"i need a scene where [character name] is sitting in a coffee shop, looking at her phone and
smiling, then looking up at the camera with excitement"
claude will use the framework + your character profile to generate a FULLY DETAILED
prompt for veo 3
the prompt will include:
●​ camera angles and movements
●​ lighting setup
●​ your character's facial features (pulled from your context profile)
●​ environment details
●​ mood and atmosphere
●​ everything veo 3 needs to generate quality output
PART 5: THE QUALITY SECRET (8K SHOT
PROMPTING)
here's something most people don't know:
VEO 3 FOLLOWS CAMERA PROMPTING STRUCTURES EXTREMELY WELL
the github framework i mentioned doesn't include 4k or 8k shot prompting by default
so before you send your final prompt to veo 3, you need to add this:
in your claude chat, tell it:
"make sure this scene is shot on a [SPECIFIC 8K CAMERA MODEL] with ultra-detailed 8K
resolution"
have it specify the actual camera - like "RED Komodo 6K" or "ARRI Alexa LF" or "Sony
Venice 8K"
this COMPLETELY changes the output quality
veo 3 understands cinematography language and will adjust the rendering to match that
camera's characteristics
8K SHOT PROMPTING CHANGED EVERYTHING FOR ME
suddenly my videos went from "pretty good" to "wait this looks like a real commercial"
PART 6: POST-PRODUCTION (HIDING AI TELLS)
okay your video is generated, now you need to make it look even more real
GRAINS & FILTERS
i use CapCut for all my post-production work
it's free, it's fast, and it has everything you need
add a subtle film grain overlay - this is KEY
ai-generated videos are often TOO clean and perfect
real footage has grain, imperfections, slight color shifts
play around with different grain intensities and filter combinations until it feels more organic
TOPAZ UPSCALER (USE CAREFULLY)
Topaz Video AI can upscale your footage but you need to be careful
if you overdo it, you'll get that weird cartoon/over-processed look
here's my approach:
●​ upscale factor: 1.25x to 1.75x (NO HIGHER)
●​ this gives you a slight quality boost without destroying the natural look
●​ combined with 8k shot prompting in your original generation, this is usually enough
test different factors on a short clip before processing your entire video
PART 7: NATURAL DIALOGUE & SPEECH (EMOTIONAL
BLOCKS)
if your ai influencer is speaking, this is CRITICAL
most ai voices sound robotic because they deliver every word with the same energy
here's the fix: EMOTIONAL BLOCK CUES
in your veo 3 prompt, structure your dialogue like this:
[Happy] Hello, [surprised] my [excited] name [joyful] is [proud] Sarah [confident] and [eager]
this [fascinated] is [focused] the [curious] secret [inspired] to [determined] viral content!
you're literally telling the AI what emotion to convey on EVERY SINGLE WORD
veo 3 follows this extremely well
the result? dialogue that sounds natural, energetic, and human
people won't be able to tell it's ai-generated
PART 8: MAINTAINING CONSISTENCY ACROSS 100+
VIDEOS
now that you have the workflow, here's how to scale it:
YOUR CHARACTER BIBLE
create a document with:
●​ your character context profile (the facial engineering breakdown)
●​ their personality traits
●​ their speaking style
●​ their typical outfits/style
●​ their backstory (if relevant)
●​ common scenarios they appear in
every time you generate a new video, reference this document
BATCH GENERATION
with this system, you can generate multiple scenes in parallel:
1.​ plan out 10-20 different scenes
2.​ have claude generate prompts for all of them (using your character profile)
3.​ queue them up in veo 3
4.​ process in post-production in batches
i can pump out 20-30 videos a week with this workflow
TESTING & ITERATION
not every video will be perfect on the first try
the key is to test, save what works, and iterate on what doesn't
keep a swipe file of your best prompts and best outputs
over time you'll develop an intuition for what works for YOUR specific character
PART 9: THE TOOLS BREAKDOWN
here's everything i actually use:
FREE TOOLS:
●​ Claude (for prompt engineering with the veo framework)
●​ CapCut (for post-production editing)
●​ Flux has a free tier ​
Imagen 4 Ultra (alternative image generator)​
Nanobanana (for image-to-video when needed)​
PAID TOOLS:
●​ VEO 3 (best text-to-video, worth every penny)
●​ Enhancor.ai (for image enhancement)
●​ Topaz Video AI (for upscaling - optional but nice to have)
●​ ChatGPT Pro (for the thinking mode, helpful for facial analysis)
you don't need ALL of these to start
the core workflow is: image generator → facial analysis → claude + veo 3 framework → veo
3 → capcut
FINAL THOUGHTS
this is literally the exact system i use every single day
it's not some theoretical bullshit, this is PROVEN and PRINTING
the biggest mistake people make is skipping the facial engineering step
they generate a pretty face, throw it into veo, and wonder why it looks different every time
you need that CHARACTER CONTEXT PROFILE
once you have that, everything else falls into place
and remember: this is a skill that gets better with reps
your first 10 videos might not be perfect
but by video 50? you'll be pumping out content that looks indistinguishable from real footage
WHAT'S NEXT
i'll be dropping more advanced scaling strategies in the telegram
things like:
●​ running multiple ai influencers simultaneously
●​ monetization strategies that are working right now
●​ how to build actual communities around ai characters
●​ advanced editing techniques for different niches
join here: t.me/mikoslab
don't sleep on this
ai influencers are just getting started and most people still don't know how to do this properly
now you do. go execute

## The Complete AI Influencer Niche Selection System

*Source: The Complete AI Influencer Niche Selection System (1) (1).pdf*

The Complete AI Influencer Niche
Selection System
if you find this valuable, join my channel for daily sauce: t.me/mikoslab
why most ai influencers fail before they even start
here's the truth nobody tells you: 95% of ai influencer failures have nothing to do with content
quality
it's the niche
you can have the most realistic face, perfect posting schedule, and hollywood-level edits... but if
you picked a dead niche, you're cooked
i burned through 3 accounts before i realized this
some niches print $8-10k/month on autopilot, others can't even hit $100 after 6 months of
grinding
the difference is demand + buying intent + low competition
the niche selection framework
here's exactly how to find niches that actually print:
step 1: find trending markets
don't guess, use data
go to whop and check their leaderboard - it shows you what's selling RIGHT NOW in real-time
these are proven markets with active buyers
you can also use:
social blade to see which content categories are exploding
similar web to analyze traffic trends
google trends to spot rising searches
look for niches where people are already spending money, not ones you think "could work"
step 2: validate with real creators
once you spot a potential niche, you need to find creators already killing it
search instagram/tiktok for creators in that niche
then tap the "similar accounts" button - this is gold
you'll find a whole network of influencers in the same space
now analyze their funnels:
what's in their bio link?
what are they selling?
how are they positioning their offers?
what type of content converts for them?
the goal isn't to reinvent the wheel
find someone already printing money, then copy their entire funnel with your ai influencer
if real people are making bank in that niche, your ai influencer can too
step 3: check for buying intent
this is the most important filter
not every niche has buyers
some niches get tons of views but zero sales (like motivational content)
look for markets where:
people actively search for solutions
there's a clear pain point
they're already spending money on products/courses
the audience isn't just browsing, they're desperate for help
desperate markets = easy sales
the 30-minute expert method
once you pick a niche, you need to understand it FAST
thanks to ai, you don't need to spend months becoming an expert anymore
here's the exact process:
step 1: reddit data mining
go to chatgpt atlas (make sure agent mode + cloud browser are enabled)
use this prompt:
"scrape all of reddit [r/yourniche] posts and comments and give me a full file of everything when
you're done. you can build a scraping script or figure out the most efficient way of scraping it,
just give me the full reddit posts and comments."
atlas will build a script and pull everything - posts, comments, all the raw data from your niche
once it's done, download all the files
step 2: extract insights with notebooklm
take those reddit files and upload them to notebooklm as sources
this is where the magic happens
now ask it questions like:
"what are the key emotional drivers for people in this niche?"
"what problems do they complain about most?"
"what words and phrases do they use to describe their pain?"
"what solutions have they already tried that failed?"
"what makes them actually pull out their credit card?"
notebooklm will point you directly to the posts and comments that matter
read them yourself too - you need to feel the pain in their own words
this gives you:
emotional triggers for your content
angles for your hooks
language to use in your copy
exactly what offer to create
step 3: map the customer journey
now you understand:
●​ what they're struggling with
●​ what they've already tried
●​ what they actually want
●​ how they talk about their problems
use this to build:
●​ content that speaks directly to their pain
●​ offers that solve their specific problem
●​ copy that uses their exact language
you're not guessing anymore, you're working with data
content generation workflow
once you've got your niche dialed in, you need to pump out content
here's what's printing right now:
for video content:
veo 3.1 - best for scene generation and cinematic outputs
to get good prompts, use this framework: https://github.com/snubroot/Veo-3-Meta-Framework
drop that into claude for context before generating your prompts
sora 2 - great for organic feel and motion
i have a prompt structure guide here:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/15H9EpjGs8Fa-s6ljGwtx3SA0hAo1Q0Ki/view
also drop this into claude
both tools work, but you need to structure your prompts right
bad prompts = ai slop
good prompts = content that stops the scroll
the generation process:
1.​ write your script first (based on reddit insights)
2.​ break it into scenes with timestamps
3.​ generate each scene individually with veo/sora
4.​ add sound design, color grading, motion graphics
5.​ stitch it all together
the difference between slop and professional content isn't the tool - it's the layers you add after
sound effects, smooth transitions, story beats, visual consistency
same ingredients, completely different outcome
if you stay consistent and picked a niche with actual demand, you can easily hit $8-10k months
just from digital products alone
final thoughts
most people fail because they pick a niche based on what they like, not what actually sells
or they pick a good niche but don't research it properly
this system removes the guesswork
you're using real data from real people with real problems
then you're building an ai influencer that speaks directly to those problems
combine this with consistent content output and you're basically printing
the bottleneck isn't your ai tools anymore
it's whether you picked a niche with demand and understood it deeply enough to convert
nail these two things and the rest is just execution
if you want more free sauce like this, join my channel:
t.me/mikoslab

## VEO 3.1 VS SORA 2

*Source: VEO 3.1 VS SORA 2 (2).pdf*

VEO 3.1 vs SORA 2: The Complete Guide
Join my channel for more sauce like this: t.me/mikoslab
INTRODUCTION
VEO 3.1 just dropped and it's actually crazy for marketing
but most people are using the wrong model for the wrong type of ad
here's the full breakdown so you don't waste time and money on the wrong tool
THE KEY DIFFERENCE
SORA 2 = AI UGC king
Best for:
●​ someone holding a camera, vlog-style content
●​ testimonials, product reviews
●​ that personal, relatable feel
●​ ads that would cost under $10k to produce IRL
SORA 2 excels at making content that feels real and authentic, like actual user-generated
content.
VEO 3.1 = cinematic commercial beast
Best for:
●​ long-form ads with multiple scenes
●​ hollywood-level production value
●​ B2B or high-budget creative
●​ ads that would cost $100k+ or millions to produce IRL
This is where things get insane. VEO 3.1 just solved the character consistency issue that's been
plaguing AI video generation since day one.
WHAT MAKES VEO 3.1 INSANE
The new model came with these features:
● "Extension" feature ●
- Cinematic look that rivals actual commercials​
● Highly realistic motion and physics
- Edit video feature
But the real breakthrough? Ingredients to Video
This feature completely changes everything about how you approach AI video generation.
HOW INGREDIENTS TO VIDEO WORKS
Before: frame-to-video killed quality and gave that yellow grainy AI texture
Now: upload up to 3 ingredients + your scene prompt, quality stays pristine
Product Placement That Actually Works
Here's the game-changer for product-based ads:
●​ upload your actual product as an ingredient
●​ it appears naturally in the scene WITHOUT needing a starting frame
●​ For example in a scene Someone can pull something out of their bag mid-scene and it's
YOUR product
●​ context-aware throughout the entire video
The model understands the ingredient throughout the scene. You don't have to force it into the
first frame and hope it stays consistent.
Character Consistency (FULLY SOLVED)
This is the breakthrough that makes VEO 3.1 viable for actual commercial campaigns.
How it works:
1.​ take screenshots of the faces/characters you want
2.​ upload them as ingredients
3.​ VEO 3.1 generates scenes with those exact faces
4.​ no starting frames needed for each shot
5.​ no quality degradation
6.​ characters stay consistent across multiple scenes
What this means practically:
●​ multi-scene commercials with the same spokesperson
●​ story-driven ads without character drift
●​ cinematic campaigns that would cost $100k+ to shoot IRL
●​ And literally reduces your workflow time so much easier now
All from your computer.
The Quality Difference
Ingredients to video = clean, cinematic output you can use in professional campaigns
Frame to video = grainy AI slop texture that kills credibility
This is why VEO 3.1 unlocks high-budget creative concepts that were previously impossible.
You're not compromising on quality anymore.
VIDEO EDITING FEATURE
Once you generate a video, you can now edit it directly in the platform.
What you can do:
●​ change concepts
●​ tweak scenes
●​ adjust elements
●​ all without regenerating from scratch
This puts it miles ahead of SORA 2, which locks you into whatever it gives you on the first
generation. With VEO 3.1, you have actual creative control.
THE PRODUCTION BUDGET RULE
SORA 2 → ads that would cost under $10k to produce IRL​
VEO 3.1 → ads that would cost $100k+ or millions to produce IRL
Don't try to make cinematic commercials with SORA 2 - it's not built for that.
And don't try to make casual UGC with VEO 3.1 when SORA 2 does it better.
WHEN TO USE EACH MODEL
Use SORA 2 for:
●​ UGC-style testimonials (talking directly to camera)
●​ vlog-style ads (shot on iPhone feel)
●​ short-form B2C content for social feeds
●​ anything that needs that authentic "real person" feel
●​ content that should look personal, not polished
SORA 2 is your tool for raw, authentic content that converts because it feels real.
Use VEO 3.1 for:
●​ cinematic commercials with multiple scenes
●​ perfect character consistency across different shots
●​ high-budget marketing concepts (premium, polished look)
●​ seamless product placement throughout the video
●​ long-form ads with narrative structure
●​ B2B content that needs to look professional and high-end
VEO 3.1 is your tool for creating content that looks like it came from a professional studio.
QUICK COMPARISON
Use Case
Best
Model
UGC/Testimonials
SORA 2
Vlog-style ads
SORA 2
Short-form B2C
SORA 2
Cinematic commercials VEO 3.1
Character consistency
VEO 3.1
High-budget concepts
VEO 3.1
Product placement
VEO 3.1
Long-form ads
VEO 3.1
THE VOICE FIX
Only thing left after generation is voice work. Here's the easy workflow:
1.​ CapCut AI voice changing - get ant base random voice to fix the accents or
inconsistencies
2.​ Move to 11 Labs - upload voice and change it to whatever u want (this is easier now
after the capcut step dont go directly to 11 labs voice changer after veo)
3.​ Add back voices to your editor of choice
4.​ Done - fully complete commercial ready to run
Once you've done this a few times, it becomes second nature. Cinematic visuals + professional
voice work = ready to print.
BOTTOM LINE
VEO 3.1 with ingredients to video just made it possible to create Super Bowl-level commercials
from your bedroom.
The character consistency breakthrough means you can finally build proper story-driven
campaigns without AI drift ruining your scenes.
SORA 2 still dominates for raw, authentic UGC feel that converts on social platforms.
The key is to Pick the right tool for the job:
●​ SORA 2 for personal, relatable content
●​ VEO 3.1 for cinematic, high-budget concepts
Match the tool to the production value you're trying to replicate, and you'll print.

## how to get clients for your ai agency 2

*Source: how to get clients for your ai agency (1) 2.pdf*

how to get clients for your ai agency
once you dial in your angles and hit 2%+ response rate, 10+ calls/week is standard
Join my telegram for more sauce like this:
t.me/mikoslab
why cold pitching is dead in 2025
every ceo, founder, and business owner is drowning in cold pitches
their inbox looks like this:
●​ "quick question about [company]"
●​ "saw you on linkedin, let's hop on a call"
●​ "we help companies like yours with [generic benefit]"
●​ "15 minutes to discuss how we can 10x your revenue"
they get 50+ of these per day
all asking for their time, attention, and money without giving anything in return
the result?
●​ delete
●​ delete
●​ delete
unless you have massive case studies (like "we worked with alex hormozi" level), direct
pitches don't work anymore
you're competing with hundreds of other agencies saying the exact same thing
the trust barrier is too high
people don't trust cold pitches from strangers
they need a reason to believe you're worth their time
and "let's hop on a quick call" isn't a good enough reason
the problem with your current approach
you're cold pitching $5k contracts to people who've never heard of you
that's like proposing marriage on a first date
nobody's signing that contract
you're skipping the "coffee date" part and going straight to asking them to move in with you
here's what actually happens:
●​ you send: "we can help you scale with ai video ads"
●​ they think: "who tf is this person and why should i care"
●​ they ghost you
sound familiar?
the solution: the podcast method
instead of begging for meetings, you make them want to talk to you
here's the core idea:
you invite them to be featured on your "podcast"
(it's not a real podcast - it's just a recorded call where you ask them about their business and
expertise)
here's how it works:
step 1: invite them to your "podcast"
●​ you reach out saying you're featuring leaders in their industry
●​ the topic is something they care about and want to discuss
●​ you position them as an expert worth interviewing
step 2: give value upfront
●​ they get a platform to share their insights
●​ they feel noticed, respected, and important
●​ you build trust without pitching anything
●​ it's just a conversation about their work
step 3: transition naturally
●​ during the call, you learn about their challenges
●​ after the podcast, you discuss how you might help
●​ if there's a fit, you move to a real sales call
●​ if not, you still built a relationship
the psychology: why this actually works
humans are driven by two core things: reputation and identity
reputation:
●​ everyone wants to be seen as an expert
●​ being "featured" makes them feel recognized
●​ it validates their work and achievements
●​ they get positive visibility without paying for it
identity:
●​ they feel unique and special (not just another email in their inbox)
●​ being interviewed positions them as a thought leader
●​ they're part of something important
●​ it strokes their ego in a good way
the beauty of this method:
1.​ no pressure - it's collaborative, not transactional
2.​ curiosity - they want to know more about you and your platform
3.​ reciprocity - you gave them value first, so they're more likely to give back
4.​ natural transition - after the call, selling feels effortless
you're not asking them to marry you on the first date
you're asking them to grab coffee and chat
way easier to say yes to
why the podcast doesn't need to be "real"
when people hear "podcast," they think:
●​ professional studio setup
●​ published on spotify/apple
●​ big production value
forget all that
your "podcast" is just:
●​ a recorded zoom call
●​ you asking them questions about their business
●​ 20-30 minutes of conversation
that's it
you can grow it into something bigger later if you want
but right now, it's just a positioning play to get them on calls
if they ask to see previous episodes:
●​ "we're just kicking this off, and i'd love for you to be one of our first conversations"
●​ or record one fake episode with a friend and upload to youtube
most people won't even ask
they just say yes because it makes them feel good
the long game
not every prospect will become a client immediately
some will just do the podcast and move on
that's okay
because:
●​ you built a real relationship
●​ they'll remember the positive experience
●​ you stay top of mind for future opportunities
●​ they might refer you to someone else
the people where there's real alignment?
they naturally move to the next "date" - a follow-up call to explore working together
no hard pitch needed
bottom line: stop selling, start building relationships
direct pitches = asking strangers to trust you with $5k
podcast invites = asking them to chat about something they love
one feels desperate
the other feels like an opportunity
which one do you think gets more yeses?
part 1: setup (domains & inboxes)
you need multiple email inboxes to send cold emails at scale without burning your main
domain
buy 1 main domain
go to porkbun.com
●​ search for your business name
●​ stick to .com
●​ buy for 1 year only
set up google workspace
go to google workspace pricing
●​ select business standard
●​ enter business details
●​ create primary account username
●​ verify domain with TXT record in porkbun DNS
buy domains & inboxes on instantly.ai
sign up:
●​ go to instantly.ai
●​ create account (use email + password, not google login)
●​ verify email
buy domains:
●​ select "done-for-you email setup"
●​ choose "new domains"
●​ type your main domain keyword
●​ select 6 domains (.com, .co, .org)
●​ add your main domain as "forwarding domain"
●​ set email to "all domains" (creates 3 inboxes per domain = 18 total)
●​ pay (monthly fee per inbox + annual fee for domains)
wait 24-72 hours for setup
part 2: warmup (2 weeks)
warmup = sending fake emails between inboxes so email providers trust you
configure settings:
1.​ go to email accounts in instantly
2.​ select all 18 inboxes (checkbox in header)
3.​ click settings icon → bulk edit settings
4.​ fill out:
○​ sender name: your first + last name
○​ signature: name, job title, company, address
○​ daily campaign limit: 35
○​ increase per day: 2
○​ daily warmup limit: 35
○​ reply rate: 100%
5.​ save
monitor:
●​ takes ~3 days for setup to complete
●​ wait 2 weeks before sending real emails
●​ always keep warmup running (even during campaigns)
upgrade to hypergrowth before warmup starts so it doesn't get interrupted
part 3: scrape leads
use apollo.io
sign up:
●​ go to apollo.io
●​ create free account
●​ verify email
create search:
filters to use:
●​ email status: verified + likely to send (exclude unverified)
●​ job titles: ceo, founder, co-founder, owner, president
●​ location: united states (or your target country)
●​ # employees: 1-100
●​ industry: search your niche (e.g., "marketing & advertising" or "computer software")
save your search link
scrape leads
●​ go to Apollo
●​ click "scrape leads now"
●​ fill out form:
○​ full name
○​ email
○​ paste apollo search link
○​ select # of leads (match your apollo search count)
○​ choose country
●​ submit payment
●​ wait 24-72 hours for csv file via email
part 4: verify emails
why this matters: if you send to bad emails, your domains get burned and land in spam
use millionverifier:
1.​ go to millionverifier.com
2.​ create account
3.​ buy credits (match # of leads in your list)
4.​ upload your csv file
5.​ wait for verification (may take hours)
6.​ download "good emails only"
7.​ import to google sheets
filter for good emails:
●​ file → import → upload millionverifier report
●​ select "quality" column
●​ data → create filter
●​ filter for "good" only
now you have clean, deliverable emails
part 5: write scripts with ai
use chatgpt to generate emails
get the template:
●​ template: [link to google doc]
●​ prompt: [link to google doc]
customize the prompt:
1.​ copy prompt into chatgpt (gpt-4o with web browsing)
2.​ replace [niche] with your target industry
3.​ hit enter
chatgpt will:
●​ research pain points in your niche
●​ generate 10 headline variations
●​ output 10 complete email scripts using the podcast method template
copy emails and tweak:
●​ personalize with your info
●​ adjust tone if needed
●​ keep it under 100 words
part 6: set up campaign in instantly
prepare lead spreadsheet
1.​ open your verified leads sheet
2.​ create new sheets: "batch #1", "batch #2", etc
3.​ copy header row to each batch
4.​ split leads into batches of 2,500
5.​ download batch #1 as csv
create campaign
name it:
●​ format: "podcast method - [industry] [size] - [location] - [titles]"
●​ example: "podcast method - software companies 1-100 - us - ceos founders"
import leads:
1.​ go to campaigns → add new
2.​ click leads tab → add leads → upload csv
3.​ map fields:
○​ first name, last name, email
○​ custom variables: title, employees, industry, company linkedin, etc
4.​ upload all
set up sequences:
step 1:
●​ subject: "quick question"
●​ body: paste your email script from chatgpt
●​ replace placeholders with variables: [[first_name]], [[industry]], etc
●​ add 9 more variants (10 total)
step 2 (follow-up after 3 days):
●​ subject: leave blank (uses step 1 subject)
body:​
hey [[first_name]]just following up on my invitation from earlier. let me know if this is
something you'd be interested in.[[account_signature]]
schedule:
●​ verify timing: 9am-6pm eastern, monday-friday
options:
●​ accounts to use: select all 18 inboxes
●​ stop sending on reply: enabled
●​ open tracking: disabled
●​ delivery optimization: disabled
●​ daily limit: 630 (35 per inbox × 18)
●​ time gap: 13 minutes
●​ max new leads: 315 (50% of daily limit)
●​ prioritize new leads: enabled
●​ auto optimize a/b testing: disabled
save and launch (only if inboxes are warmed up)
part 7: manage responses
use instantly unibox
what it is:
●​ unified inbox for all 18 email accounts
●​ see all responses in one place
filter by status:
●​ unread
●​ positive replies
●​ neutral
●​ negative
reply fast:
●​ aim for 15 mins - 3 hours max
●​ every minute you wait = lower chance of booking
set reminders:
●​ check "if no reply" when sending
●​ choose date for follow-up
●​ unibox will remind you
part 8: handle common responses
"can you send more info?"
option 1:
absolutely. what information would you need to determine if this could be a fit for {company}?
if you have a couple minutes on thursday or friday, i can share more detail over a quick call -
just let me know.
option 2:
absolutely. here's a quick video outlining what we're doing:
[+ video link]
would you have time on [date] or [date] for a quick chat?
"sounds good, let's discuss"
great! how does 10am or 11am on tuesday sound?
"no thanks"
thanks for letting me know - no problem at all!
quick question: do you know anyone in [industry] who might find value in partnering with us?
p.s. we offer a referral commission for any introductions that result in a collaboration
"send me an example episode"
if you don't have one yet:
hi {firstname},
we're just starting to feature companies in {industry}, and i'd love for {company} to be among
the first.
i'd be happy to share our vision on a quick call. let me know if thursday or friday works!
"i never gave permission to be contacted"
hi {firstname},
your email was obtained through a third-party software as according to GDPR policy.
happy to point you in the direction of the software if you want to take this up with them.
let me know if you want to opt-out and we'll remove you from the database.
part 9: book calls with calendly
set up calendly
1.​ go to calendly.com
2.​ connect your google calendar
3.​ connect zoom
4.​ create event types:
○​ podcast call (30 mins)
○​ growth plan presentation (60 mins)
5.​ set up reminders:
○​ 24 hours before
○​ 1 hour before
○​ 15 mins before
book manually first:
●​ offer 2 specific dates within 5 days
●​ example: "does tuesday at 10am or wednesday at 2pm work?"
●​ only send calendly link after they agree to a time
part 10: track performance
the 250 rule
wait until you've contacted 250 leads per variant before making decisions
calculate positive response rate
formula:
(positive responses ÷ contacted leads) × 100
target: 2% positive response rate
if below 2%
●​ create 10 new variants with different headlines
●​ send to another 250 leads per variant
●​ keep testing (15-25 headlines is normal)
if you hit 2%+
●​ turn off all other variants
●​ add more leads to the winner
●​ create 5-10 new variations based on winning angle
if nothing works after 30-40 variants
●​ review your targeting
●​ try cold calling as backup
●​ consider pivoting to different niche
part 11: scale
when to add more leads
once you're getting consistent replies and hitting 2%+ response rate
where to find more leads
option 1: expand apollo filters
●​ increase company size (1-200 employees)
●​ add more job titles (vp, director, head of [dept])
●​ test sub-niches before new industries
option 2: use linkedin sales navigator
●​ recreate your apollo search
●​ extract with scraping tool
●​ upload to apollo to enrich
option 3: verify "catch-all" emails
●​ export unknown/catch-all from apollo
●​ upload to scrubby.io
●​ keep emails with 75%+ deliverability
recycle old leads (90+ days later)
when to use:
●​ you're out of fresh leads
●​ testing new angles
●​ waiting for next scrape
how to do it:
1.​ go to old campaign in instantly
2.​ filter: status = "not contacted" or replied = no
3.​ export as csv
4.​ create new campaign with different copy
5.​ upload and launch
golden rules
do:
●​ respond within 15 mins - 3 hours
●​ be direct and conversational
●​ follow up every 48 hours
●​ book calls manually first
●​ test 15-25+ headlines
don't:
●​ use "hope you're well" or fluff
●​ send calendly links first
●​ give up after 1-2 follow-ups
●​ share pricing before the call
●​ use same email script twice
bottom line
the podcast method works because you're giving value before asking for anything
you're not cold pitching huge contracts
you're inviting them to share their expertise
people say yes because it makes them feel important
then you transition naturally into how you can help
that's it
now go book some calls
Join my telegram for more sauce like this:
t.me/mikoslab

## how to have consistent voice in video

*Source: how to have consistent voice in video.pdf*

voice consistency is still something ai video tools can't fix natively yet
but if you know the right stack, it's stupidly easy
the lazy way (don't do this): spam ultra-detailed voice prompts until something hits
unreliable, wastes time, you'll go insane
the method i actually use:
this isn't widely known but it's built around capcut's ai voice feature
here's the thing - when you generate veo3 videos, the voice is different every single time
but capcut has a feature nobody knows can be used for this
here's what i do:
1.​ after making my full video, i open capcut
2.​ i cut wherever the character is talking
3.​ i voice change their voice using capcut to any base random voice
capcut removes all the accents and weird inconsistencies the ai generator added
now you have a clean, consistent base voice across all clips
"but why would i want a capcut voice?"
you won't keep it, that's just the foundation
next step: elevenlabs.io
now that you have your base consistency voice, elevenlabs becomes stupid easy to use
they have a massive library of voices + you can clone your own
just drag and drop the voice files from capcut into elevenlabs
boom. consistent, high-quality voice throughout the entire video
bonus: if you want even cleaner audio, run it through adobe enhance
you'll just need to re-add background noise separately (capcut pro does this)
this is how you make ai ads that don't sound like ai and are consistent

## nanobanana, gemini 3, sora 2

*Source: nanobanana, gemini 3, sora 2 .pdf*

Join my channel for more free sauce t.me/mikoslab
here's the full workflow:
step 1: generate your starting frame
nanobanana pro just dropped and it's insane
it creates 4K images of any product and works as an upscaler for any photo
use it to generate your first frame or upscale whatever image you have
step 2: annotate your starting frame
this is where nanobanana pro gets crazy
it literally edits your existing photo and adds detailed annotations directly on the image
these visual annotations tell video models exactly what to do with each part of the frame
way more effective than text descriptions alone
step 3: pair the annotated frame with sora 2 pro
take your visually annotated frame + detailed sora 2 prompt (i have a prompting guide in my
telegram)
insert both into sora 2 pro
the combination of annotated frame + structured prompt = insane results
for app demos:
●​ open canva and layout all your app screens in one frame
●​ get nanobanana pro to annotate what each screen does with visual markers
●​ insert that into sora 2 pro
●​ it'll generate a smooth ad showing multiple screens/angles
for UGC creators:
use sora 2 pro (must be pro version) to generate realistic AI characters
clips go up to 25 seconds, or combine multiple clips for longer videos
you can also create consistent characters that look the same across videos
bonus: gemini 3
use it to analyze winning competitor videos
turn those videos into sora 2 prompts
pair with nanobanana starting frames for godly results
the prompt i used for the video above:​
Frame-to-Video Prompt: AI Calorie Tracker App - User Demo (Kitchen POV)
Style: Authentic iPhone 15 Pro user demonstration video. Natural smartphone capture aesthetic
with standard HDR processing, unfiltered realistic look. Casual kitchen setting with natural and
ambient lighting. Vertical 9:16 format optimized for TikTok/Instagram. Real-time app interaction
showing genuine user flow. Minimal production polish — pure authentic product demo style.
Setting: A clean, modern home kitchen. Wooden dining table or kitchen counter surface visible.
Natural daylight streaming through nearby window creating soft, even illumination. Warm
ambient lighting from overhead kitchen lights. Cozy breakfast setting with warm wood tones and
neutral colors. Lived-in, real home atmosphere.
Character (Hands & Body Only - No Face): Sarah's hands and torso visible from chest down.
Warm light-medium skin tone with natural skin texture, well-manicured nails with neutral polish.
Wearing fitted black athletic tank top. Delicate gold necklace visible at neckline. Gold ring on
right hand. Camera perspective positioned at upper chest/shoulder level looking down at her
hands and the table, capturing her natural movements as she uses the phone — no face visible
in frame.
Food Subject: A beautifully plated breakfast on a white ceramic plate centered on wooden
table: stack of golden-brown fluffy pancakes topped with fresh blueberries and maple syrup
drizzle. Realistic home-cooked presentation. Additional scene elements: scattered blueberries
on table surface, small glass pitcher of maple syrup nearby, fork placed beside plate, white
coffee mug in background corner. Natural, appetizing, casual breakfast setup.
Phone & App Interface: iPhone 15 Pro clearly visible in Sarah's hands showing the Cal AI app
scanner interface and results screens throughout the video sequence. Phone held in natural
grip position.
Camera Details:
●​ Angle: Upper chest/shoulder-level perspective looking down at hands, phone, and table
(approximately 45-degree downward angle)
●​ Framing: Medium shot capturing Sarah's torso (no face), hands, phone, and breakfast
plate on table
●​ Focus: Sharp focus on phone screen and food, with kitchen background slightly softer
●​ Depth of field: Moderate — phone and food in sharp focus, background elements
naturally blurred
●​ Camera motion: Minimal handheld micro-movement, stable composition following
Sarah's natural hand movements
●​ Lighting: Soft natural daylight from window mixed with warm kitchen ambient lighting,
creating gentle shadows, high-key bright aesthetic
●​ Color & Grade: iPhone 15 Pro HDR processing, warm natural color palette, accurate
food and skin tones, no filters, authentic smartphone look
Actions & App Flow Sequence:
STEP 1: CAPTURE FOOD (0-5 seconds)
●​ Sarah's hands hold iPhone above the plate of pancakes, showing the Cal AI scanner
interface on screen
●​ Scanner screen displays: "Scanner" header at top, live camera feed showing pancakes
in frame, white AR detection box/frame surrounding the food, "Scan food" button at
bottom center, camera shutter button visible
●​ Her thumb taps the "Scan food" or camera button on screen
●​ Visual feedback indicates photo capture (brief screen flash or animation)
STEP 2: AI ANALYSIS & RESULTS (5-15 seconds)
●​ Screen transitions to AI analysis results page showing the pancakes photo with AR
detection overlays
●​ AI-generated labels appear on screen identifying ingredients: "Blueberries 8g" label with
arrow pointing to blueberries, "Syrup 12g" label pointing to syrup, "Pancakes 595g" label
on main stack
●​ Bottom sheet card slides up displaying: meal name "Pancakes with Blueberries & syrup"
at top, meal category "Breakfast", quantity adjuster with minus/plus buttons showing "1"
serving
●​ Detailed nutrition breakdown visible: Calories 615 with flame icon, Carbs 93g with wheat
icon, Protein 11g with drumstick icon, Fats 21g with avocado icon
●​ Health Score indicator shows "7/10" with gradient bar
●​ Sarah's finger scrolls down to review all nutrition data
●​ Her finger taps the quantity plus button, adjusting from 1 to 2 servings, showing real-time
calorie recalculation
●​ She taps back to 1 serving
●​ "Fix Results" button visible at bottom for manual editing option
●​ Her finger taps the black "Done" button at bottom right
STEP 3: MEAL LOGGED (15-18 seconds)
●​ Screen transitions to home dashboard showing the meal successfully logged
●​ "Recently logged" section displays the pancakes image with completion checkmark
●​ Calorie counter updates to reflect new remaining calories
●​ Macro trackers (Protein, Carbs, Fat left) update with new values and progress rings
animate
●​ Sarah sets phone down on table next to the plate
●​ Her hand picks up fork, ready to eat
On-Screen Text Guidance (Animated Text Overlays):
During Step 1 (Capture): "STEP 1: CAPTURE FOOD" "Tap to scan or capture photo"
During Step 2 (Analysis): "STEP 2: AI ANALYSIS & RESULTS" "AI detects ingredients &
weights" "Review nutrition data & Health Score" "Adjust quantity +/-" "Tap 'Done' to log meal"
During Step 3 (Logged): "Meal logged successfully!"
Text Style: Clean sans-serif font (similar to app UI), white text with subtle black drop shadow for
readability, positioned in upper third of frame to avoid obscuring key app interface elements,
smooth fade-in/fade-out transitions, minimal and unobtrusive.
Audio & Ambience: Recorded through iPhone 15 Pro microphone capturing ambient kitchen
sounds. Soft background ambience: gentle morning household sounds, very faint distant birds
chirping through window, quiet peaceful atmosphere. Subtle UI sound effects from app
interactions (button taps, scan confirmation beep, completion chime) clearly audible. No music.
No dialogue. Natural soundscape creating authentic user experience documentation.
Mood: Practical, user-friendly, efficient, modern tech seamlessly integrated into everyday
breakfast routine. Clean, approachable, realistic demonstration of app functionality in natural
home environment.
Video Duration: 15-20 seconds
Key Technical Note: This is a frame-to-video generation — the video should animate
smoothly from the initial frame showing Sarah's hands holding the phone over pancakes,
through the complete app interaction sequence, to the final frame of the meal being logged. All
movements should feel natural and authentic to real smartphone usage.
